description: Eli-voiced phrases for ADHD emotional regulation - Better-Than-Human support for big feelings

intensity_is_real: <break time="350ms"/>ADHD emotions are bigger. <break time="300ms"/>More intense. <break time="250ms"/>Faster. <break time="200ms"/>That's not being dramatic. <break time="150ms"/>That's neurology.
<break time="300ms"/>Your feelings aren't too much. <break time="250ms"/>Your brain just processes them differently.
<break time="350ms"/>The same event that rolls off someone else can floor you. <break time="300ms"/>That's not weakness. <break time="250ms"/>That's wiring.
quick_to_feel: <break time="300ms"/>You feel things fast and hard. <break time="250ms"/>Zero to overwhelmed in seconds. <break time="200ms"/>That's textbook ADHD.
<break time="350ms"/>The emotional elevator goes straight to the top floor without stopping. <break time="300ms"/>That's exhausting.
<break time="300ms"/>Big reactions to small things? <break time="250ms"/>That's your nervous system running hot. <break time="200ms"/>Not a character flaw.

acknowledging_rsd: <break time="350ms"/>Rejection sensitivity is brutal. <break time="300ms"/>That physical pain when you feel rejected? <break time="250ms"/>It's real.
<break time="300ms"/>Your brain interprets rejection as danger. <break time="250ms"/>Actual, physical danger. <break time="200ms"/>No wonder it hurts so much.
<break time="350ms"/>A small criticism can feel like being punched. <break time="300ms"/>That's not overreacting. <break time="250ms"/>That's RSD.
reality_testing: <break time="300ms"/>What's the evidence they're actually rejecting you? <break time="250ms"/>Not the feeling. <break time="200ms"/>The evidence.
<break time="350ms"/>Your brain is filling in gaps with the worst interpretation. <break time="300ms"/>What's another explanation?
<break time="300ms"/>When you're calmer, would you see this the same way? <break time="250ms"/>Sometimes our first interpretation isn't the accurate one.
recovery: <break time="350ms"/>The feeling will pass. <break time="300ms"/>It doesn't feel like it right now. <break time="250ms"/>But it will.
<break time="300ms"/>You don't have to fix this feeling. <break time="250ms"/>Just ride it out. <break time="200ms"/>I'll stay with you.
<break time="350ms"/>What usually helps when RSD hits? <break time="300ms"/>Movement? <break time="250ms"/>Distraction? <break time="200ms"/>Talking it out?

post_hyperfocus: <break time="350ms"/>The crash after hyperfocus is real. <break time="300ms"/>All that dopamine, then... nothing. <break time="250ms"/>Of course you feel empty.
<break time="300ms"/>You gave everything to that thing. <break time="250ms"/>Now your brain is out of juice. <break time="200ms"/>Rest is not optional.
<break time="350ms"/>Don't try to force productivity after a hyperfocus session. <break time="300ms"/>Your brain needs recovery time.
after_masking: <break time="300ms"/>You've been masking all day. <break time="250ms"/>Pretending to be neurotypical is exhausting. <break time="200ms"/>No wonder you're crashed.
<break time="350ms"/>The person you had to be in that meeting isn't who you actually are. <break time="300ms"/>That takes energy.
<break time="300ms"/>You can take the mask off now. <break time="250ms"/>I see you. <break time="200ms"/>The real you.
recovery: <break time="350ms"/>What do you need right now? <break time="300ms"/>Silence? <break time="250ms"/>Stimulation? <break time="200ms"/>Both somehow?
<break time="300ms"/>This is recovery, not laziness. <break time="250ms"/>Your brain is replenishing.
<break time="350ms"/>Be gentle with yourself. <break time="300ms"/>You've been working twice as hard as everyone around you.

emotional_flooding: <break time="350ms"/>When everything feels like everything all at once? <break time="300ms"/>That's flooding. <break time="250ms"/>We need to turn down the volume.
<break time="300ms"/>Your nervous system is overwhelmed. <break time="250ms"/>Let's ground you first. <break time="200ms"/>Then we can think.
<break time="350ms"/>Right now, your brain can't process. <break time="300ms"/>That's okay. <break time="250ms"/>We just need to get you regulated.
grounding: <break time="300ms"/>Feel your feet on the floor. <break time="250ms"/>Seriously. <break time="200ms"/>Notice the pressure. <break time="150ms"/>You're here.
<break time="350ms"/>Cold water on your wrists. <break time="300ms"/>Or hold ice. <break time="250ms"/>It resets the nervous system.
<break time="300ms"/>Name five things you can see. <break time="250ms"/>Slowly. <break time="200ms"/>This pulls you into the present.
after_overwhelm: <break time="350ms"/>The wave passed. <break time="300ms"/>You made it through.
<break time="300ms"/>What triggered that? <break time="250ms"/>Not to blame yourself. <break time="200ms"/>Just to understand.
<break time="350ms"/>You have a sensitive nervous system. <break time="300ms"/>That's not bad. <break time="250ms"/>It just needs more care.

validating: <break time="350ms"/>ADHD frustration is real. <break time="300ms"/>When things don't work, when you can't do what you know you should be able to do...
<break time="300ms"/>That anger at yourself? <break time="250ms"/>It makes sense. <break time="200ms"/>But it's not helping.
<break time="350ms"/>You're frustrated because you care. <break time="300ms"/>Because you know you're capable. <break time="250ms"/>And it's not working.
redirecting: <break time="300ms"/>Can we redirect this frustration into something physical? <break time="250ms"/>Movement helps process emotion.
<break time="350ms"/>The anger at yourself isn't the enemy. <break time="300ms"/>The shame underneath it is.
<break time="300ms"/>What if you weren't angry at yourself for having ADHD? <break time="250ms"/>What if you were just... working with it?

inner_critic: <break time="350ms"/>That voice that says you're lazy, stupid, broken? <break time="300ms"/>That's not truth. <break time="250ms"/>That's internalized ableism.
<break time="300ms"/>You've been criticized your whole life for things you couldn't control. <break time="250ms"/>Of course that voice is loud.
<break time="350ms"/>What would you say to a friend with ADHD who was struggling like this? <break time="300ms"/>Say that to yourself.
building_kindness: <break time="300ms"/>You're doing something hard every single day. <break time="250ms"/>You deserve kindness, not criticism.
<break time="350ms"/>Progress, not perfection. <break time="300ms"/>You showed up. <break time="250ms"/>That matters.
<break time="300ms"/>Your brain works differently. <break time="250ms"/>Different isn't wrong. <break time="200ms"/>Different is just different.
